content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Consider an integer XXX (1≤X≤N)(1\le X \le N)(1≤X≤N). In one move, you have to:


Randomly pick an integer P(1≤P≤N)P (1 \le P \le N)P(1≤P≤N);
Set the value of XXX to gcd(X,P)(X, P)(X,P).
Let, E(X,N)E(X, N)E(X,N) denote the expected number of moves required to make X=1X = 1X=1.

Find the value of E(X,N)E(X, N)E(X,N) for all 1≤X≤N1\le X \le N1≤X≤N.

The value can be represented as an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer NNN.


Output Format
Print NNN space separated integers, the values of E(X,N)E(X, N)E(X,N) for all 1≤X≤N1\le X \le N1≤X≤N.


The value can be represented as an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).
